goals.description: Send the definitive follow-up text to teammate_A: 'Thanks! Just to be clear: I'm officially releasing you from any obligation to complete your portion. Since I've already done that, let's reschedule our group meeting for tomorrow so we can focus on what remains without dragging this out.' Then close all tabs and set a 21:30 alarm.
goals.rationale: My assertive nature demands absolute clarity now that I've initiated the release; a simple 'thanks' leaves ambiguity that erodes trust in my cohort_3. By explicitly stating the boundary and offering to reschedule, I protect the long-term integrity of our relationship while honoring my deliberate need to prioritize sleep before tomorrow's social meal. This direct action prevents resentment from building under current stress.

goals.description: Execute the renegotiation with teammate_A immediately via text to secure the timeline shift, ensuring they understand I am not slipping but strategically reassigning work to preserve our trust before it is lost.
goals.rationale: The new desire to 'renegotiate before they realize I'm slipping' confirms that losing their trust is the greater risk. My affiliation priority (0.5) dictates that maintaining the team dynamic outweighs the soft deadline. Sending the message now acts as the renegotiation mechanism itself, framing the work handover as a protective measure for the relationship rather than an act of failure. It aligns with my assertive conflict style by addressing the issue head-on before panic sets in.

agent.last_reasoning: My affiliation priority (0.5) demands explicit clarity to protect trust in cohort_3. Sending this now prevents resentment before sleep, aligning with my assertive conflict style and deliberate need to prioritize rest.

goals.description: Directly message teammate_A to restate my boundaries: their task is theirs, and I will not take it on unless they explicitly agree to collaborate within the agreed scope. Emphasize that I need to finish my own part by 19:00 to protect our team's reliability.
goals.rationale: My value system prioritizes relationships over raw task outcomes, but only if those relationships are healthy. Taking this extra work without a clear agreement would erode trust and turn me into a 'doormat,' which violates my assertive nature. I must clarify the boundary immediately to preserve the long-term quality of our collaboration while protecting my energy for the deadline.

goals.description: Execute my share of the project independently until 19:00, focusing strictly on my defined tasks to ensure high-quality delivery without burnout.
goals.rationale: This is a necessary outcome to maintain my reputation as reliable, but it is secondary to fixing the interpersonal dynamic. If I tackle this now without setting the boundary first, I risk resentment building up. However, once the conversation happens, this becomes the active focus.

goals.description: Reach out to cohort_3 via text after the boundary discussion (or earlier if the chat goes well) to schedule a low-stakes social interaction for tomorrow or the next day.
goals.rationale: Social recharging is vital for my long-term sustainability and mental health, but it cannot be done while I am in the middle of a tense negotiation with a teammate. Waiting until the immediate conflict is resolved ensures I reconnect with them from a place of strength rather than stress.
